mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate increases the cost of capital and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when the federal funds rate is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts below the trailing twelve-month average signals deteriorating residential investment, which precedes broader demand contraction and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its trailing 12-month moving average
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative in at least 60% of instances where housing starts fall below their trailing 12-month average.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration of consumer price inflation and deceleration of payroll employment growth signals stagflationary pressure that erodes corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL accelerates and PAYEMS decelerates simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime with the federal funds rate above 4%, an acceleration in unemployment over the prior three months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because tighter credit amplifies labor market weakness into earnings declines.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over the prior three months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE accelerates during monetary tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive earnings expectations and S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, the Federal Reserve is compelled to maintain monetary tightening, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production confirm a coordinated contraction in final demand and output, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when both RSAFS and INDPRO decline month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of unemployment below 5%, month-over-month payroll employment growth signals a tight labor market that sustains wage-driven consumption and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of months when PAYEMS grows and UNRATE is below 5%.
